name: External Control of Critical State Data
description: The product stores security-critical state information about its users or itself in a location that is accessible to unauthorized actors. If an attacker can modify this state data (stored in cookies, hidden form fields, environment variables, or configuration files), they can perform unauthorized actions or access unexpected resources.
detection_pattern: STORAGE node holding security-critical state (session data, authorization flags, configuration) is accessible from INGRESS without AUTH mediation. Look for state stored client-side (cookies, hidden fields, localStorage) that controls security decisions without server-side integrity verification.
